questions.question:
Consider three processes, all arriving at time zero, with total execution time of \( 10, 20 \) and \( 30\; \text{units,} \) respectively. Each process spends the first \( 20 \% \) of execution time doing \( \text{I/O}, \) the next \( 70 \% \) of time doing computation, and the last \( 10 \% \) of time doing \( \text{I/O} \) again. The operating system uses a shortest remaining compute time first scheduling algorithm and schedules a new process either when the running process gets blocked on \( \text{I/O} \) or when the running process finishes its compute burst. Assume that all \( \text{I/O} \) operations can be overlapped as much as possible. For what percentage of time does the \( \text{CPU} \) remain idle?



Operating System: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 61

In an operating system, processes that are terminated but, for some reason must have its task structure in the process table are referred as __________.



Operating System: NIELIT Scientist B 2020 November: 86
